frameworks.name: multiscale computation
frameworks.one_line: adaptive integration of information across local and global scales in time and space

frameworks.name: dynamic attention
frameworks.one_line: modulation of integration scale to balance computation cost and explore-exploit

frameworks.name: prefrontal cognitive control
frameworks.one_line: dACC and dlPFC implement top-down scale modulation across hierarchies

frameworks.name: transformer and attention architectures
frameworks.one_line: AI systems with dynamic scale modulation contrasted with fixed-scale RNN/CNN

core_question: How do biological brains and AI systems adaptively modulate integration across multiple scales to support hierarchical goals?
key_assumptions: intelligence requires integrating information across scales in time and space
scale modulation jointly addresses efficiency and explore-exploit
manipulations: review across decision inertia, habit formation, information search, risky choice, foraging domains
comparison of fixed-scale RNN/CNN against attention/transformer/dynamic convolution architectures
key_findings: dorsal anterior cingulate and dorsolateral prefrontal cortex represent and modulate across scales
AI progress tracks innovations from fixed scaling to dynamic multiscale modulation
interpretations: biological and artificial multiscale modulation share architectural principles
dynamic scale modulation resolves computational inefficiency and explore-exploit simultaneously
